item.title: Demystifying Portfolio Reviews
item.description:
Tuesday, October 28, 2025 at 5 PM

Location: Online Event via Zoom

Join us online on Tuesday, October 28, from 5:00-6:00pm CST for an informative free webinar covering everything you need to know about portfolio reviews. Having the perspective of both photographer and reviewer, HCP Managing Director of Education Natan Dvir will review important guidelines, discuss best practices, and provide valuable tips and resources.Registration is required using this link.

From Houston Center for Photography

Event details

item.title: Artist Panel Conversation: HCP Fellowship Exhibitions and Fractured Sublime
item.description:
Thursday, November 20, 2025 at 5:30 PM

Location: 1441 W Alabama St, Houston, TX 77006

Join us for an Artist Panel Conversation featuring this year’s HCP Annual Fellowship recipients Emma Ressel and Sasha Arutyunova alongside juror Gregory J. Harris and HOU @ HCP gallery artist Daniel Seth Kraus. The dialogue will be facilitated by HCP Exhibitions & Programs Coordinator, Areli Magallón and immediately followed by an opening reception.

From Houston Center for Photography

Event details

item.title: Opening Reception: HCP Fellowship Exhibitions and Fractured Sublime
item.description:
Thursday, November 20, 2025 at 6:15 PM

Location: 1441 W Alabama St, Houston, TX 77006

Join us Thursday, November 20, between 6:15pm to 8pm CST for the opening reception of the annual Fellowship Exhibitions, including Extant Erosions by Emma Ressel and Wind and Other Dangers by Sasha Arutyunova, both juried by Gregory J. Harris, High Museum of Art’s Donald and Marilyn Keough Family Curator of Photography, alongside Fractured Sublime by Daniel Seth Kraus. Guests will have the opportunity to purchase signed copies of exhibiting artists’ books.

From Houston Center for Photography

item.title: Why Images Matter: Truth, Empathy, Connection
item.description:
Wednesday, December 3, 2025 at 6:30 PM

Location: 1441 W Alabama St, Houston, TX 77006

Join us on Wednesday, December 3, between 6:30pm to 8:00pm CST for an in-person presentation of Sara Terry’s and Louie Palu’s extraordinary work followed by an engaging conversation led by Lisa Volpe, Curator of Photography at the Museum of Fine Art, Houston (MFAH). This program is made possible thanks to the generous support of Joan Morgenstern and also serves as a tribute to the late Sara Terry, whose recent passing is felt deeply across the photographic community. Sara’s extraordinary commit

Thursday, December 18, 2025 at 6 PM

Location: 1441 W Alabama St, Houston, TX 77006

Foto Futures‍, a Community Engagement program, is a springboard for high school students who may be interested in pursuing a higher education degree in photography and/or related media.

item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: 1441 W Alabama St, Houston, TX 77006

item.title: August Critique Group: Howard Bossen
item.description:
Sunday, August 9, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: 1441 W Alabama St, Houston, TX 77006

HCP Members are invited to join our June Critique Group led by Howard Bossen, Professor Emeritus of Photography and Visual Communication, School of Journalism at Michigan State University. You may register for free as a Presenter and get feedback on your work from Castro and fellow HCP members, or as an Attendee and partake in the discussion. Our critique groups provide a supportive and professional environment to share your work and take the next step. Please visit our Critique Groups page for

From Houston Center for Photography
